content: Hey coach, how am I doing this week overall?

You're doing reasonably well - your workout consistency has been solid most days, but your water intake has dipped below your 2.5L target on a few occasions. Productivity has been strong, especially your coding sessions. I'd focus on keeping a bottle nearby so water doesn't slip through the cracks.
Yeah water is my weak point honestly. Any tips?

A few that actually work: keep a marked 1L bottle at your desk and aim to finish it twice by evening, set a recurring reminder every 2 hours, and pair water with something you already do daily - like a glass right after each meal. Small consistent habits beat trying to chug 2L in one sitting.
makes sense. what about my workout - am i doing pushups too much and neglecting legs?

Looking at your recent logs, yes - pushups and pullups show up far more often than squats or leg-focused work. That's a common pattern when working out at home without equipment, but it'll create imbalance over time. Try alternating - upper body one day, lower body/core the next, even if it's just bodyweight squats and lunges.
noted. also i skipped logging context for like 3 days, does that mess up my scores?

Not directly - context isn't scored, it just helps me judge your other scores more fairly. Without it, if your numbers dip I have less explanation to go on, so I score more conservatively. If something disrupted your routine on those days, it's worth logging even after the fact so future reviews have the full picture.
fair enough. Am I ready to increase my productivity target?

Based on the last couple weeks you're hitting 60 minutes on most days without much strain, with a few 90+ minute sessions. I'd bump the target to 75 minutes rather than jumping straight to 90 - a smaller increase is more sustainable and less likely to make you dread logging it. You can always raise it again once 75 feels easy.
